_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Conduct AI risk assessments to ensure models and/or other solutions are performing as designed.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting AI impact assessments, including risk tiering, harm identification, bias evaluation, human oversight documentation, and residual risk determination.
comments: DCWF task equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-S-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-T-012 (RC, cosine: 0.737), AG-T-008 (AG, cosine: 0.731).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.8/10 (raw 0.582). Shared vocabulary: [ai, assessments, risk]. DCWF-only terms [conduct, designed, ensure, models, performing, solutions] vs WIDAI-only terms [bias, conducting, determination, documentation, evaluation, harm] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.866 (P=0.863, R=0.869). Balanced P/R — the 14-word DCWF element and 20-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.6%). "Conduct AI risk assessments to ensure models and/or other solutions are performi" and "Skill in conducting AI impact assessments, including risk tiering, harm identifi" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.751 > STS 0.582 (gap +0.169). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, assessments, risk] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 13.3%. Shared tokens: [ai, assessments, risk]. DCWF-only: [conduct, designed, ensure, models, performing, solutions]. WIDAI-only: [bias, conducting, determination, documentation, evaluation, harm].

signals.detail: STS 0.582 classified as Equal but closer to Superset threshold. Classification may be over-generous.

Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.169 with low STS 0.582. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
